We are given the matrix:

$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
-\frac{4}{3} & 4 & -\frac{11}{6} \\
-\frac{13}{3} & \frac{23}{6} & -\frac{25}{6} \\
\frac{5}{3} & -\frac{2}{3} & -\frac{5}{3}
\end{pmatrix}
$$

We are to find the **determinant** of this 3×3 matrix.

---

### Step 1: Use the cofactor expansion (along the first row)

The determinant of a 3×3 matrix:

$$
\text{det}(A) = a_{11} \cdot \text{det}\begin{pmatrix} b_{22} & b_{23} \\ b_{32} & b_{33} \end{pmatrix}
- a_{12} \cdot \text{det}\begin{pmatrix} b_{21} & b_{23} \\ b_{31} & b_{33} \end{pmatrix}
+ a_{13} \cdot \text{det}\begin{pmatrix} b_{21} & b_{22} \\ b_{31} & b_{32} \end{pmatrix}
$$

Where:

$$
a_{11} = -\frac{4}{3}, \quad a_{12} = 4, \quad a_{13} = -\frac{11}{6}
$$

and the submatrices are:

- $ b_{22} = \frac{23}{6}, \quad b_{23} = -\frac{25}{6}, \quad b_{32} = -\frac{2}{3} $
- $ b_{21} = -\frac{13}{3}, \quad b_{23} = -\frac{25}{6}, \quad b_{33} = -\frac{5}{3} $
- $ b_{21} = -\frac{13}{3}, \quad b_{22} = \frac{23}{6}, \quad b_{31} = \frac{5}{3} $
